job_description:
Some Careers Shine Brighter Than Others.If you’re looking for a career that will help you stand out, join HSBC and fulfil your potential. Whether you want a career that could take you to the top, or simply take you in an exciting new direction, HSBC offers opportunities, support and rewards that will take you further.HSBC is one of the largest banking and financial services organisations in the world, with operations in 64 countries and territories. We aim to be where the growth is, enabling businesses to thrive and economies to prosper, and, ultimately, helping people to fulfil their hopes and realise their ambitions.We are currently seeking an experienced professional to join our team in the role of Processor Cash Payms and RecsPurpose Of The RolePayment Investigations deals with the handling and resolution of queries received from customers and banks related to payments that have already been processed. Responsible for handling Investigations on PEGA Platform, Pass payments adjustments entries on client accounts, internal accounts & Nostro accounts. Investigating and identify root cause for an inward or outward payment enquiry and ensure timely resolutionIn This Role, You Will Responsible to contact external banks through SWIFT correspondence messages as and when required. Independently investigate and identify root cause for an enquiry. Independently review Nostro accounts & ensure all outstanding debits & credits are cleared as per timelines.  Responsible to follow up with external banks for details, confirmation and recall of funds. Review and verify Correspondence & Payment adjustments in accordance with the currency cut-off. Acquire and update knowledge on procedures and products.  Customers (External/ Internal) Receive/make calls from/to customers (internal/ external) Delight internal and external customers. Responsible to independently process customer instructions accurately to achieve agreed quality standards and targets (as amended or notified from time to time) on all the processes worked.  Internal Control Environment: Identify and escalate potential complaints. Responsible to undertake work without any Ops Losses (Actual, Potential or Near Misses) due to errors. Proactively escalate to the Line Management any issues / transactions that would impact service delivery or potential loss to the bank.  Leadership & Teamwork Provide efficient resolution to customers while on call and clarity of thought Ensure error free and quality processing. Good team player Liaise with peers and colleagues for timely resolution.  Understand critical documentation and interpreting information and risk involved. To Be Succeed In This Role Previous experience in the banking or finance industry. Knowledge of Swift payments and able to decode all Message Types (MT and MX) is desired. Knowledge of Payments processing and Payments Investigation’s case handling is desired.  Proven track record of strong focus towards high levels of Quality and Customer Service Strong Relationship building skills as requires coordination with onshore/offshore teams in day-to-day activities.  Others: Ability to multi-task, depending on the criticality of the tasks. Self-motivated and should be able to work under minimal supervision. Flexibility to work in different shifts including weekends.  Good communication skills – Written & Verbal Attention to detail and Quick Learner. You’ll achieve more at HSBC www.hsbc.com/careers HSBC is committed to building a culture where all employees are valued, respected and opinions count. We take pride in providing a workplace that fosters continuous professional development, flexible working and opportunities to grow within an inclusive and diverse environment. Personal data held by the Bank relating to employment applications will be used in accordance with our Privacy Statement, which is available on our website.Issued By HSBC Electronic Data Processing (India) Private LTD***

job_description_raw_html:
Some Careers Shine Brighter Than Others.

If you’re looking for a career that will help you stand out, join HSBC and fulfil your potential. Whether you want a career that could take you to the top, or simply take you in an exciting new direction, HSBC offers opportunities, support and rewards that will take you further.

HSBC is one of the largest banking and financial services organisations in the world, with operations in 64 countries and territories. We aim to be where the growth is, enabling businesses to thrive and economies to prosper, and, ultimately, helping people to fulfil their hopes and realise their ambitions.

We are currently seeking an experienced professional to join our team in the role of Processor Cash Payms and Recs

Purpose Of The Role

Payment Investigations deals with the handling and resolution of queries received from customers and banks related to payments that have already been processed. Responsible for handling Investigations on PEGA Platform, Pass payments adjustments entries on client accounts, internal accounts & Nostro accounts. Investigating and identify root cause for an inward or outward payment enquiry and ensure timely resolution

In This Role, You Will

  • Responsible to contact external banks through SWIFT correspondence messages as and when required. Independently investigate and identify root cause for an enquiry. Independently review Nostro accounts & ensure all outstanding debits & credits are cleared as per timelines.
  • Responsible to follow up with external banks for details, confirmation and recall of funds. Review and verify Correspondence & Payment adjustments in accordance with the currency cut-off. Acquire and update knowledge on procedures and products.
  • Customers (External/ Internal) Receive/make calls from/to customers (internal/ external) Delight internal and external customers. Responsible to independently process customer instructions accurately to achieve agreed quality standards and targets (as amended or notified from time to time) on all the processes worked.
  • Internal Control Environment: Identify and escalate potential complaints. Responsible to undertake work without any Ops Losses (Actual, Potential or Near Misses) due to errors. Proactively escalate to the Line Management any issues / transactions that would impact service delivery or potential loss to the bank.
  • Leadership & Teamwork Provide efficient resolution to customers while on call and clarity of thought Ensure error free and quality processing. Good team player Liaise with peers and colleagues for timely resolution.
  • Understand critical documentation and interpreting information and risk involved.

To Be Succeed In This Role

  • Previous experience in the banking or finance industry. Knowledge of Swift payments and able to decode all Message Types (MT and MX) is desired. Knowledge of Payments processing and Payments Investigation’s case handling is desired.
  • Proven track record of strong focus towards high levels of Quality and Customer Service Strong Relationship building skills as requires coordination with onshore/offshore teams in day-to-day activities.
  • Others: Ability to multi-task, depending on the criticality of the tasks. Self-motivated and should be able to work under minimal supervision. Flexibility to work in different shifts including weekends.
  • Good communication skills – Written & Verbal Attention to detail and Quick Learner.

You’ll achieve more at HSBC

 www.hsbc.com/careers

HSBC is committed to building a culture where all employees are valued, respected and opinions count. We take pride in providing a workplace that fosters continuous professional development, flexible working and opportunities to grow within an inclusive and diverse environment. Personal data held by the Bank relating to employment applications will be used in accordance with our Privacy Statement, which is available on our website.

  • Issued By HSBC Electronic Data Processing (India) Private LTD***

job_description_raw_html:
About The Role & Team

We are looking for an exceptional Chief of Staff to the CEO who will work at the center of Meesho’s leadership system - partnering closely with the CEO and CXOs/GMs to drive company-wide strategy, operating rigor, and high-impact cross-functional initiatives.

This is not a support role - it is a leadership role.

The Chief of Staff will act as a force multiplier for the CEO - helping evolve the company’s operating system, driving mission-critical strategic projects, shaping culture, and increasing the velocity and quality of executive decision-making. The role will continuously evolve as the company’s priorities evolve.

This is a front-row seat to building and scaling a public technology company - and requires extreme ownership, structured thinking, execution speed, and leadership maturity.

What You’ll Do

  • CEO Leverage & Executive Partnership
  • Act as a strategic partner and thought partner to the CEO
  • Help prioritize, structure, and drive the CEO’s highest-impact initiatives
  • Improve CEO decision velocity and clarity through structured inputs and analysis
  • Prepare strategic briefs, decision frameworks, and executive narratives
  • Represent the CEO in key forums and internal leadership discussions when needed
  • Company Operating System & Governance
  • Help design and continuously evolve the company’s operating cadence and management systems
  • Drive cross-functional governance rhythms (reviews, planning cycles, performance tracking)
  • Improve clarity, alignment, and accountability across leadership teams
  • Ensure follow-through on CEO and leadership priorities
  • Strategic & Cross-Functional Projects
  • Lead and execute high-impact, cross-org strategic initiatives
  • Work on projects that cut across business, product, tech, and operations
  • Incubate and evaluate new business opportunities and adjacencies
  • Structure ambiguous problems and convert them into executable plans
  • Work closely with CXOs and GMs to unblock and accelerate key priorities
  • Culture & Leadership Influence
  • Help reinforce leadership principles and cultural expectations
  • Drive leadership communications and alignment initiatives
  • Influence how decisions get made and how leaders collaborate
  • Help raise the bar on thinking quality and execution discipline
  • Role Characteristics
  • Scope will evolve with company priorities and CEO agenda
  • High ambiguity, high ownership, high trust role
  • Requires comfort operating without formal authority but with high influence
  • Combines strategy, execution, governance, and leadership leverage
  • Mix of thinker + builder + operator


What You’ll Need

  • Must Have
  • 5-7 Years of work experience
  • Strong structured problem solving and executive communication skills
  • Demonstrated experience working on complex, ambiguous business problems
  • Proven ability to drive cross-functional execution
  • High ownership and bias toward action
  • Comfort working with senior leadership teams
  • Strong Advantage
  • Experience building or scaling tech products or businesses
  • Startup or high-growth company operating experience
  • Prior experience in founder’s office / CEO office / strategy roles
  • Experience working closely with product and technology teams
  • Exposure to marketplace, e-commerce, or platform businesses
  • Leadership Traits We Value
  • Hungry, high-drive leader who thrives in fast-paced, high-intensity environments with strong first-principles thinking and high learning velocity
  • Low ego, high accountability, operating with discretion and sound judgment
  • Exceptional clarity of thought and communication, with strong influence without authority
  • Highly trusted by senior stakeholders for reliability, judgment, and impact
  • Why This Role Matters
  • Direct impact on CEO effectiveness and company trajectory
  • Opportunity to shape how a public tech company operates and scales
  • Exposure to the most important strategic decisions in the company
  • Platform to build enterprise-level leadership capability
  • Launchpad for future leadership roles within the organization
